hints.title: Account
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapters 8.9.1 and 8.9.2)

You will usually find the IBAN on your bank statement, in your online banking or on your bank card.

When receiving unemployment benefit and Citizen's benefit at the same time, it is technically not possible to receive these benefits into two different accounts.

If you do not have an account, you can apply for one at a financial institution (bank/savings bank). Financial institutions are generally legally obliged to set up a basic account for every citizen. A refusal is only possible in special exceptional cases.

hints.title: Personal identification numbers
hints.text:
A personal identification number is needed to identify a person.

You will find the pension/social insurance number on your social insurance card. Alternatively, you can ask the pension insurance fund or health insurance fund for the pension/social insurance number.

Persons registered in Germany receive a (tax) identification number. You will find it in your income tax return or pay slip. Alternatively, you can ask the Federal Central Tax Office for the (tax) identification number.

If your country of origin has assigned a national identification number, please provide it and attach proof.

hints.title: Formal declaration of commitment
hints.text: A person can commit to the immigration authority or foreign mission to cover all costs that arise during your stay in Germany. This is called a formal declaration of commitment. To assess the entitlement, submit the declaration of commitment. If you do not have the declaration of commitment, please submit other suitable documents, for example details of the person who financially supports you or who should support you according to the declaration.

hints.title: Application
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapter 4.1)

Your application usually takes effect retroactively from the first of the month in which you submit an application (§ 37 paragraph 2 sentence 2 SGB II). We therefore need your information for the entire month of your application. You can also apply for the benefits only from a certain point in time, for example for the following month.

hints.title: Ability to work
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapter 8.1.1)

Persons who can work at least three hours a day are able to work.

Persons for whom employment cannot temporarily be expected are also considered able to work, for example if a person
• is ill for no longer than 6 months,
• is pregnant,
• is raising a child under 3 years of age,
• cares for relatives in need of care, or
• attends school.

An entitlement to benefits under SGB II only exists if at least one person in your benefit community is able to work.

hints.title: School/study/training
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapter 8.8.4)

You may be entitled to benefits under the Federal Training Assistance Act (BAföG), vocational training allowance (BAB) or training allowance (ABG) if you attend a vocational school, study or do an apprenticeship. If an entitlement exists, you must also make use of it. Your Jobcenter may ask you to apply for BAföG/BAB/ABG if you have not yet submitted an application and your training is eligible for support.

In any case, suitable proof is required. This can be, for example, the school certificate, the certificate of enrolment/de-registration or the training contract. If you use a training contract as proof, you may redact information that is not required.

Accommodation away from home during training exists if the trainee lives in a hall of residence, a boarding school, a barracks or a special facility for people with disabilities. Or if they live with the trainer with full board or in an apartment with cost reimbursement for accommodation and meals.

hints.title: School books/workbooks
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapter 8.6)

The costs for school books and workbooks can be covered if the purchase was required by the school or the respective teacher. The required school books and workbooks must not be provided free of charge by the school or by third parties, for example a school support association. Please attach corresponding proof from the school and a receipt. The proofs and receipts must each contain the ISBN numbers of the school books and workbooks.

In addition, the costs for a paid loan of these school books and, where applicable, workbooks can be covered in the amount of the personal contribution.

hints.title: Benefit community
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapter 8.2)

By submitting the application, you form a benefit community. In addition, the following persons belong to your benefit community:
• the spouse,
• the registered civil partner,
• the partner ("marriage-like community") who lives with you,
• your unmarried children and those of the persons named above who have not yet reached the age of 25 and live in the household.

If you, as the applicant, are between 15 and 24 years old, then your parents or parent living in the household belong to your benefit community. However, if you have your own child and/or are married, then your parents or parent living in the household do not belong to your benefit community.

hints.title: Persons entitled under the Asylum Seekers Benefits Act
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapter 8.1)

Persons entitled under the Asylum Seekers Benefits Act cannot receive benefits under SGB II.

Proof of your current residence status by means of a residence permit, residence authorisation or toleration is stored as a copy in the Jobcenter's files.

If you use the decision of the Federal Office for Migration and Refugees as proof, only the pages on which the legal basis and validity for the recognition or granting of subsidiary protection under the Residence Act are visible are required. You do not need to submit the entire decision.

hints.title: Claims against third parties
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapter 13)

Claims against third parties can be, for example:
• contractual payment claims, for example from a purchase contract, rental contract or loan,
• compensation claims (recourse),
• claims against employers (outstanding wage or salary payments),
• claims from unjust enrichment,
• claims from inheritances,
• reclaim claims from gifts,
• claims from a transfer or retirement provision contract,
• claims from an occupational pension scheme, or
• unfulfilled, contractually secured annuity payments.

hints.title: Additional need for pregnant women
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapter 8.6)

You can prove a pregnancy, for example, with a medical certificate or by presenting the maternity record. No copy is taken for the file. Costs may arise for a medical certificate. The Jobcenter does not cover these costs.

hints.title: Additional need for a costly diet
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapter 8.6)

If you require a costly diet for health reasons, a certificate from your attending doctor is necessary. For this you can use the medical certificate on annex MEB or present a medical attestation. The medical attestation must show the illness and the prescribed diet. The fees for issuing the attestation can be reimbursed to you upon request to a reasonable extent (currently 5.36 euros).

hints.title: Unavoidable special need
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapters 8.6 and 8.8.1)

Costs arising from special life circumstances can be covered upon request if you cannot cover these costs through savings or in another way, for example through benefits from the health/long-term care insurance fund.

These are, for example:
• permanently required hygiene products for certain illnesses, for example HIV, neurodermatitis,
• costs for exercising access rights for separated parents.

These costs do not include expenses that are already included in the Citizen's benefit, for example dentures. In special cases, an interest-free loan can be granted.

A corresponding medical attestation is sufficient as proof of an illness. If you have concerns about disclosing your illness to the caseworker, you can hand them the documents in a sealed envelope. This is then forwarded to the Jobcenter's Medical Service, which issues an opinion on the additional need without naming the specific illness. As proof of the costs for exercising access rights, please submit, for example, a decision of the youth welfare office or court or the agreement with the separated parent.

hints.title: Residential institution
hints.text: If you are staying in an institution, please state the type of institution, for example hospital, preventive care or rehabilitation facility, prison. Please also state the expected duration of the stay if this is known or can be estimated. Please submit proof of your information.

hints.title: Health and long-term care insurance
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapter 11.1)

The Jobcenter must ensure that you and the members of your benefit community have health and long-term care insurance, even if you were not previously insured. For this, the Jobcenter needs information from you about your previous health and long-term care insurance.

At the start of receiving Citizen's benefit, you can independently choose a statutory health insurance fund. If you wish to exercise this right of choice, please present a membership certificate or other proof of the chosen health insurance fund within two weeks, ideally at the same time as your application for Citizen's benefit. Otherwise you will be compulsorily insured with your previous health insurance fund. In this case, the last electronic health card can be presented as a copy instead. The copy is not taken for the file.

Privately insured persons are generally entitled to a subsidy towards the health and long-term care insurance contributions, provided that statutory health and long-term care insurance is not possible. Please provide proof of the amount of the private health insurance contributions. The document must show whether these correspond to the contributions of your individual basic tariff. If you are not insured in the basic tariff, please additionally provide proof of the contributions of that tariff. The subsidy is generally transferred directly to the respective health insurance fund. Please therefore provide the bank details of your health insurance fund.

If you have further questions, please contact a health insurance fund.

hints.title: Household community
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapter 8.2)

The household community includes, for example, the following persons who live with you in the household:
• relatives and in-laws (grandparents, uncles, aunts) and/or
• foster children and foster parents.

Please fill out annex HG separately for each person who lives in a household with you.

A pure shared flat is neither a benefit community nor a household community, so annex HG does not need to be filled out.

hints.title: Community of responsibility and mutual support
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapter 8.2)

If a community of responsibility and mutual support exists, the income and assets of the partner are taken into account. A community of responsibility and mutual support exists if you live together in a partnership in a shared household and bear responsibility for one another and support one another.

Bearing responsibility for one another and supporting one another is presumed if you:
• live together for longer than one year,
• live together with a common child,
• jointly care for children or relatives in the household, or
• are authorised to dispose of the other person's income or assets.

Other facts can also establish the existence of a community of responsibility and mutual support. This can be, for example, a given promise of marriage, living in jointly owned property, or the actual care of a partner in the shared household. The Jobcenter may request further data from you or from the person with whom you (presumably) form a partnership for this purpose.

hints.title: Assets
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapter 9.4)

Please state the assets of all persons in the benefit community in annex VM. This also includes assets abroad. The responsible Jobcenter assesses whether assets are realisable.

Assets include, for example:
• (online) bank and savings balances, cash, securities, shares, bonds, cryptocurrency,
• receivables,
• motor vehicles, for example car, motorcycle,
• capital life insurance, private pension insurance, building society contracts,
• developed or undeveloped land, home ownership, for example single- or multi-family houses, condominiums, and
• other assets, for example valuables, paintings, jewellery.

In certain cases, the Jobcenter can request proof for inspection.

hints.title: Bank statements
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapter 14)

When you submit proof, you may generally redact, i.e. make unrecognisable, certain personal data. This includes, for example, information about ethnic origin, political opinions, beliefs, trade union membership, health or sex life (Article 9 paragraph 1 GDPR). Please note that the option to redact only exists for expenditure entries and not for income. Only certain passages of the recipient and of the booking text for expenditure entries may be redacted. The entry must remain comprehensible for the Jobcenter.

Bank statements may be stored as copies in the Jobcenter's files if their content directly affects the eligibility requirements for the benefits you have applied for under SGB II. Otherwise they are destroyed in a data-protection-compliant manner.

hints.title: Income
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapter 9.1)

Income is all earnings in money and, in certain cases, also benefits in kind, for example free meals. Please therefore state all income and submit corresponding proof. Income includes in particular the earnings named in annex EK under section C. For further income, entries can be made in the checkbox "Other and/or irregular income".

hints.title: Additional need for disability and mark G
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapter 8.6)

The mark G can be proven by presenting the severely disabled person's pass. No copy of this is taken for the file. However, it is documented.

hints.title: Market value of real estate
hints.text: Purchase contracts or market value appraisals (each as a copy) that are no older than three years are accepted as proof. If no such documents are available, information can be obtained from the responsible land registry and surveying offices.

hints.title: Holiday job
hints.text: A holiday job is an activity carried out by pupils during the school holidays.

hints.title: Expense allowances/flat-rate expense payments
hints.text: Expense allowances are payments (cash benefits/benefits in kind) that you receive when carrying out a voluntary or charitable activity to compensate for your efforts and the expenses incurred in connection with the activity. They are usually paid from public funds on the basis of public-law regulations. Typical examples are activities as a trainer in a club. You can usually list the expenses incurred in the course of a voluntary or charitable activity in keyword form. Please provide proof of the expenses. Where clients are identifiable from the proof, this information can be made unrecognisable.

hints.title: Work-related expenses/deductions
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapter 9.2)

Work-related expenses are expenses incurred in connection with income from dependent employment. An amount of 100.00 euros is disregarded as a flat rate (the so-called basic deduction amount).

Further expenses that are deducted as work-related expenses are in particular:
• expenses in connection with your employment, for example travel costs by public transport or by motor vehicle between home and workplace at 0.20 euros per distance kilometre, work equipment, meals,
• maintenance payments,
• parental income that is taken into account in a child's training support,
• expenses for legally required insurance, for example motor vehicle liability insurance,
• private insurance of minor children. For reasonable private insurance for minor children, a flat rate of 30.00 euros per month is deducted from the minor child's income.

The determination of the deduction amounts is always done individually.

hints.title: Other housing costs
hints.text: Other housing costs are understood to mean costs that are not listed in the rental contract. Electricity costs, cable fees and telephone costs are generally not eligible.

hints.title: Debt interest for home ownership
hints.text: You can prove the debt interest incurred, for example, by presenting an annual account statement or an interest and repayment plan. Information that is not required can be made unrecognisable.

hints.title: Additional need for decentralised hot water generation
hints.text:
(SGB II information leaflet, chapter 8.6)

If hot water is generated decentrally, for example via an instantaneous water heater, boiler or gas heater, an additional need for decentralised hot water generation is granted. Therefore, to assess the entitlement, be sure to fill out the "Energy sources" section in annex KDU.

hints.title: Proof of maintenance claims
hints.text:
Proof of maintenance claims are, for example:
• judgments/decisions/settlements of a court
• notarial deeds
• documents of the youth welfare office
• out-of-court maintenance agreements
• interim orders

Only if, during an assessment of the benefit entitlements, it is recognised that maintenance claims could pass to the Jobcenter upon actual granting of benefits under SGB II, the documents necessary to pursue the claims are taken for the file. In the case of a divorce judgment or divorce decision, only the presentation of the specific maintenance title is required.

hints.title: Representative
hints.text: A representative in maintenance proceedings can be a lawyer, a legal adviser, a carer or the youth welfare office.
